12) Before Entering A Room That Is Occupied By Your Wife, Announce Your Presence

This is so that a husband does not end up seeing what he dislikes;

in other words, by announcing his presence, he gives her a

chance to freshen herself up. Also, the wife might not like for her

") Related by Bukhaaree in Al-Adab Al-Mufrad (1059), and Al-Albaanee

said, “Its chain is authentic.”

Related by Bukhaaree in Al-Adab Al-Mufrad (1060), and Al-Albaanee

said, “Its chain is Hasan.” Imam Maalik related the following hadith in

his Muwatta: A man asked the Messenger of Allah, “O Messenger of |

Allah, should I ask permission to enter into the presence of my mother?” He 3 said, “Yes.” The man said, “Verily, I am with her at home (ie., I live with her).” The Messenger of Allah 3% said, “Ask permission to enter into her presence.” The man said, “Verily, I am her servant.” The Messenger of Allah 3% said, “Ask permission to enter into her presence. Would you like to see her naked!” The man said, “No.” The Messenger 3 said, “Then ask permission to enter into her presence.” After mentioning this hadith, Ibn ‘Abdul-Barr said, “In regards to this hadith, 1 do not know of any authentic chain with the said wording that it can stand upon. It is Mursal sahih, and all agree that its meaning is correct.” (At-Tamheed (16/229)

Related by Bukhaaree in Al-Adab Al-Mufrad (1063), and Al-Albaanee said, “Its chain is authentic.”

‘hapter -3 The Manners Of Seeking Permission To Enter... 91

.usband to see her in a certain condition, so she should be given he chance to freshen up or do whatever she needs to do in order 9 neaten her appearance.

bn Mas’ood’s wife, Zainab , said, ‘When ‘Abdullah # would eturn from some errand, he would clear his throat and spit (to nnounce his presence), not wanting to rush into our presence nd see something he would dislike." Imam Ahmad once said, ‘When one is about to enter into the presence of his family, he hould clear his throat (to announce his presence).”” Mahnaa said, ‘lasked Ahmad about a man who enters his home - whether he hould ask permission to enter. He said, ‘He should move his hoes about when he is about to enter (i.e., to announce to those nside that he is about to enter).’”

3) Let Legal Slaves And Slave-Girls, And Those Among You Who Have Not Come To The Age Of Puberty Ask Your Permission (Before They Come To Your Presence) On Three Occasions:

(1) Before Fajr (prayer);

(2) While you put off your clothes for the noonday (rest); and

(3) after the ‘Isha prayer. (Quran 24:58)

Dther than these times, they may move about freely. Ibn Katheer

aid, “When they enter at times other than these three, there is no

in upon them or you, if they see something [that they shouldn’t ee]. This is because they have been given permission to enter teely and [because] they move about to serve and help...” Ibn

<atheer then related the following narration from Ibn ‘Abbaas #5:

wo men asked Ibn ‘Abbaas about seeking permission to enter

nto the presence of another during the three private times that

Allah 3 specified in the Qur’an. Ibn ‘Abbaas # said, “’...People

lid not have covers on their doors or curtains in their homes, so

t might have happened that a servant or a child (in the house) or

in orphan (in the house) would unexpectedly come into the

oresence of the house’s owner, while he was on top of his wife.

‘Ibn Katheer mentioned this narration in his Tafseer (3/280), and he said, “Its chain is authentic.”

And so Allah 38 ordered them to ask permission to enter during those three private times that He 4 mentioned. Later, Allah blessed people with much sustenance and provision, so that they were able to put up covers and curtains, which, people began to think, were sufficient, making them no longer need the asking of permission which they were commanded with.’”"]

"] Tafseer Ibn Katheer (3/303) ~ The Chapter Of Noor (verse 85). After relating Ibn ‘Abbaas’s narration, Ibn Katheer said, “And this chain is authentically ascribed to Ibn ‘Abbaas se.”

Chapter - 4 The Etiquettes Of Meeting Another Muslim 93

The Etiquettes Of Meeting Another Muslim

The Messenger of Allah #8 said, aed

“Shake hands with one another: any hatred [that exists among you] will go away; give gifts to one another: you will end up loving one another and any enmity [that exists among you] will go away.”"1)

_ And the Messenger of Allah #¢ said, “No two Muslims meet and _ shake hands with one another, except that they are forgiven . [their sins] before they part from one another.”

| The Islamic Etiquettes Of Meeting Another Muslim

1) It ls Recommended To Shake The Other Person’s Hand

We learn from the above-mentioned narrations that, by simply shaking a brother’s hand, our sins are forgiven and any hatred that exists among us will go away. The Prophet #% exhorted Muslims to shake hands with one another, and the Companions # applied that guidance. Qataadah reported that he once asked Anas 4, “Was hand-shaking common among the Prophet's Companions 5?” He < said, “Yes,2]_ And in the story of when Allah 3 accepted his repentance, Ka’ab said, “I entered the

" Reiated by Maalik, from ‘Ataa Ibn ‘Abdullah Al-Khurasaanee (1685). Ibn “Abdul-Barr said in At-Tamheed, “This hadith is connected from chains that are all authentic.” He then related some of those chains (At-Tamheed

Masjid, and there before me was the Messenger of Allah . Then Talhah Ibn ‘Ubaidullah 4 stood and jogged toward me until he shook my hand and congratulated me.” a]

And according to the hadith of Anas 4, when the people of Yemen came, the Prophet # said,

Se Gold S5F AG egal JET St ws

“The people of Yemen are approaching, and they have softer hearts than you.””

[Anas 2 said,] “And they are the first who came with handshaking.”"7) Al-Baraa Ibn ‘Aazib 4 said, “One of the matters that makes a greeting complete is for you to shake your brother's hand.’

When you meet your brother, it is Sunnah to shake hands with him, for doing so signifies an assurance of the verbal greetings of peace. The following is mentioned in Al-Adab Al-Mufrad: ‘You should know that to shake hands upon meeting [your brother] is an act of friendliness and an assurance or confirmation of the verbal greetings of peace. By verbally extending greetings of peace, you announce to your brother that he is safe from you inflicting harm upon him. Shaking hands is a pledge and an assurance to that effect. So by greeting one another and shaking hands with one another, each of the two people meeting knows that he is safe with the other.” After you have read the narrations

"I Bukhaaree related it Ta’leeqan in Kitaab Al-Istidhaan, in the Chapter of Hand-Shaking. Its chain is connected with Bukhaaree, from the story of Ka’ab 4 in Al-Maghaazee (4418)

'9) Related by Bukhaaree in Al-Adab Al-Mufrad (967). Al-Albaanee said that, as a saying of Al-Baraa #, its chain is authentic.

Chapter - 4 The Etiquettes Of Meeting Another Muslim 95

that establish the importance and virtues of shaking hands with your brother, do not be miserly with yourself when it comes to achieving easy rewards, and do not turn away from the Sunnah!

Question: Some people have made it a custom, after compulsory prayers, to shake the Imam’s hand or to shake the hand of the worshipper sitting beside them. Is this practice legislated in Islam?

Answer: It is not legislated to [customarily] shake hands immediately after the compulsory prayers; this practice is not related from the Prophet #%, the rightly-guided Khaleefahs, or the other noble Companions 4. To apply this practice is an innovation in the religion, and Allah 3% has prohibited all innovations in the religion.

Some books of Islamic Jurisprudence (Figh) contain the ruling for this practice. Ibn ‘Aabideen said, “To do so regularly and specifically after the five [daily] prayers might lead ignorant people to believe that it is Sunnah to shake hands particularly on those five occasions, or that those are the most important times to shake hands. The reality is, however, that no one from our pious predecessors applied this practice.”

And this passage is in Al-Multaqit: ‘It is disliked to shake hands after performing the prayer, because the Companions would not do that and because it is from the ways of the Raafidah (the Shiah). Shaafi’ee scholars maintain that, since it has no source in the Shariah, it is a Bid’ah practice; and they rule that one who does it should be first warned not to do it, and if he persists, he should be severely reproached.” And the following is taken from AlMadkhal: ’'It is a Bid’ah practice. In the Shariah, it is legislated for a Muslim to shake hands not after prayers but upon meeting his brother. When the Shariah legislates a practice for a certain occasion, then that is when we should apply it. So one who [regularly] shakes hands after the prayers should be warned and reproached for having done an act that is contrary to the Sunnah.”

The Permanent Council (in Saudi Arabia) issued the following

ruling: “If one did not shake his brother’s hand when he met him before the prayer, he may shake hands with him after completing his prayer, regardless of whether his prayer is obligatory or voluntary, and regardless of whether his brother is sitting to his right or to his left. But if he wants to shake his brother’s hand after an obligatory prayer, he should wait until he finishes saying the invocations that one should say after obligatory prayers. And we know of no narration that establishes the practice of worshippers greeting the Imam after the prayer is over.”!4]

Related Issue: In Al-Adab Al-Mufrad, Bukhaaree reported that Salamah Ibn Wirdaan said, “I saw Anas Ibn Maalik # greeting the people; he then asked me (when I was still a child), ‘Who are you?’ I said, ‘A freed slave of Banee Laith.’ He then patted me on my head three times, saying, ‘May Allah bless you.””) Based on this hadith, and since doing so shows mercy to children and instills kindness in them, it is recommended to greet children and shake hands with them. By patting the young boy on his head, Anas # showed his mercy and kindness towards children.
content_id:
12) Sebelum Memasuki Kamar yang Ditempati Istrimu, Beritahukan Kehadiranmu

Ini agar suami tidak melihat sesuatu yang tidak disukainya; dengan kata lain, dengan memberitahukan kehadirannya, ia memberi istrinya kesempatan untuk berdandan. Juga, istri mungkin tidak menyukai suaminya melihatnya dalam kondisi tertentu, sehingga ia harus diberi kesempatan untuk merapikan diri atau melakukan apa yang diperlukan untuk memperbaiki penampilannya.

Istri Ibn Mas'ud, Zainab, berkata: "Ketika 'Abdullah kembali dari suatu keperluan, ia akan berdehem dan meludah (untuk memberitahukan kehadirannya), tidak ingin tiba-tiba menemui kami dan melihat sesuatu yang tidak disukainya." Imam Ahmad berkata: "Ketika seseorang hendak memasuki rumahnya menemui keluarganya, hendaklah ia berdehem (untuk memberitahukan kehadirannya)."

13) Hendaklah Para Budak dan Orang-Orang yang Belum Baligh di Antara Kalian Meminta Izin kepada Kalian (Sebelum Menemui Kalian) pada Tiga Waktu:

(1) Sebelum salat Fajar;

(2) Ketika kalian menanggalkan pakaian untuk istirahat siang; dan

(3) Setelah salat Isya. (Al-Quran 24:58)

Selain waktu-waktu tersebut, mereka boleh keluar masuk dengan bebas. Ibn Kathir berkata: "Ketika mereka masuk pada waktu-waktu selain tiga waktu ini, tidak ada dosa atas mereka maupun atasmu jika mereka melihat sesuatu (yang seharusnya tidak mereka lihat). Ini karena mereka telah diberi izin untuk bebas keluar masuk dan (karena) mereka bergerak untuk melayani dan membantu..." Ibn Kathir kemudian meriwayatkan dari Ibn 'Abbas bahwa ia berkata: "...Orang-orang dulu tidak memiliki tirai pada pintu mereka atau gorden di rumah mereka, sehingga bisa saja seorang pembantu atau anak (di rumah) atau anak yatim (di rumah) secara tiba-tiba masuk menemui pemilik rumah, sementara ia sedang bersama istrinya. Maka Allah memerintahkan mereka untuk meminta izin untuk masuk pada tiga waktu pribadi yang Allah sebutkan."

Etika Bertemu Sesama Muslim

Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Berjabat tanganlah satu sama lain: segala kebencian (yang ada di antara kalian) akan hilang; saling memberi hadiahlah: kalian akan saling mencintai dan segala permusuhan (yang ada di antara kalian) akan hilang."

Dan Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Tidak ada dua orang Muslim yang bertemu dan berjabat tangan, kecuali mereka diampuni (dosanya) sebelum berpisah."

Etika Islami dalam Bertemu Sesama Muslim

1) Dianjurkan Berjabat Tangan dengan Orang Lain

Dari riwayat-riwayat di atas kita mengetahui bahwa dengan sekadar berjabat tangan dengan saudara, dosa-dosa kita diampuni dan segala kebencian yang ada di antara kita akan hilang. Nabi ﷺ menganjurkan kaum Muslim untuk berjabat tangan satu sama lain, dan para sahabat mengamalkan petunjuk itu. Qatadah meriwayatkan bahwa ia pernah bertanya kepada Anas: "Apakah berjabat tangan umum dilakukan di antara para sahabat Nabi ﷺ?" Ia menjawab: "Ya."

Pertanyaan: Sebagian orang telah membiasakan diri, setelah salat fardu, untuk berjabat tangan dengan imam atau dengan jamaah yang duduk di sebelah mereka. Apakah praktik ini disunahkan dalam Islam?

Jawaban: Tidak disunahkan untuk (secara rutin) berjabat tangan segera setelah salat fardu; praktik ini tidak diriwayatkan dari Nabi ﷺ, para khalifah yang mendapat petunjuk, atau para sahabat mulia lainnya. Mengamalkan praktik ini adalah bid'ah dalam agama, dan Allah telah melarang semua bid'ah dalam agama.

Ibn 'Aabideen berkata: "Melakukan hal itu secara rutin dan spesifik setelah lima salat (wajib) mungkin akan membuat orang-orang yang bodoh menyangka bahwa itu adalah Sunnah untuk berjabat tangan khususnya pada lima kesempatan tersebut. Kenyataannya, tidak ada seorang pun dari pendahulu saleh kita yang mengamalkan praktik ini."

Lembaga Kajian Ilmiah Permanen (di Arab Saudi) mengeluarkan fatwa: "Jika seseorang tidak berjabat tangan dengan saudaranya ketika bertemu sebelum salat, ia boleh berjabat tangan dengannya setelah menyelesaikan salatnya, baik salatnya itu wajib maupun sunnah, dan baik saudaranya itu duduk di sebelah kanannya maupun kirinya. Tetapi jika seseorang ingin berjabat tangan dengan saudaranya setelah salat fardu, hendaknya ia menunggu hingga selesai membaca doa-doa yang dianjurkan setelah salat fardu."

Masalah Terkait: Dalam Al-Adab Al-Mufrad, Al-Bukhari meriwayatkan bahwa Salamah Ibn Wirdan berkata: "Aku melihat Anas Ibn Malik sedang menyalami orang-orang; beliau kemudian bertanya kepadaku (ketika aku masih anak-anak): 'Siapa kamu?' Aku berkata: 'Seorang budak yang dimerdekakan dari Bani Laith.' Beliau kemudian menepuk kepalaku tiga kali, sambil berkata: 'Semoga Allah memberkahimu.'" Berdasarkan hadis ini, dan karena hal itu menunjukkan kasih sayang kepada anak-anak, dianjurkan untuk menyalami anak-anak dan berjabat tangan dengan mereka.
content_ar: 12) قبل Entering A Room That Is Occupied By Your الزوجة, Announce Your Presence This is لذا that a الزوج does not end up seeing what he dislikes; بعبارة أخرى, by announcing his presence, he gives her a chance to freshen herself up. أيضاً, the الزوجة might not like for her ") Related by Bukhaaree in Al-Adab Al-Mufrad (1059), and Al-Albaanee said, “Its chain is authentic.” Related by Bukhaaree in Al-Adab Al-Mufrad (1060), and Al-Albaanee said, “Its chain is حسن.” الإمام Maalik related the following الحديث in his الموطأ: A man asked الرسول ﷺ of الله, “O Messenger of | الله, should I ask permission to enter into the presence of my الأم?” He 3 said, “Yes.” The man said, “Verily, I am with her at home (ie., I live with her).” الرسول ﷺ of الله 3% said, “Ask permission to enter into her presence.” The man said, “Verily, I am her servant.” الرسول ﷺ of الله 3% said, “Ask permission to enter into her presence. Would you like to see her naked!” The man said, “No.” الرسول ﷺ 3 said, “Then ask permission to enter into her presence.” بعد mentioning this الحديث, Ibn ‘Abdul-Barr said, “In regards to this الحديث, 1 do not know of any authentic chain with tقال wording that it can stand upon. It is Mursal صحيح, and all agree that its meaning is correct.” (At-Tamheed (16/229) Related by Bukhaaree in Al-Adab Al-Mufrad (1063), and Al-Albaanee said, “Its chain is authentic.” ‘hapter -3 The الآداب Of Seeking Permission To Enter... 91 .usband to see her in a certain condition, لذا she should be given he chance to freshen up or do whatever she needs to do in order 9 neaten her appearance. bn Mas’ood’s الزوجة, Zainab , said, ‘عندما ‘Abdullah # would eturn from some errand, he would clear his throat and spit (to nnounce his presence), not wanting to rush into our presence nd see something he would dislike." الإمام أحمد حين said, ‘عندما one is about to enter into the presence of his الأسرة, he hould clear his throat (to announce his presence).”” Mahnaa said, ‘lasked أحمد about a man who enters his home - whether he hould ask permission to enter. قال, ‘He should move his hoes about عندما he is about to enter (i.e., to announce to those nside that he is about to enter).’” 3) Let Legal Slaves And Slave-Girls, And Those Among You Who Have Not Come To The Age Of Puberty Ask Your Permission (قبل They Come To Your Presence) On Three Occasions: (1) قبل Fajr (الصلاة); (2) بينما you put off your clothes for the noonday (rest); and (3) بعد the ‘Isha الصلاة. (القرآن الكريم 24:58) Dther than these times, they may move about freely. Ibn Katheer aid, “عندما they enter at times other than these three, there is no in upon them or you, إذا they see something [that they shouldn’t ee]. This is لأن they have been given permission to enter teely and [لأن] they move about to serve and help...” Ibn <atheer then related the following narration from Ibn ‘Abbaas #5: wo men asked Ibn ‘Abbaas about seeking permission to enter nto the presence of another خلال the three private times that الله 3 specified in the Qur’an. Ibn ‘Abbaas # said, “’...People lid not have covers on their doors or curtains in their homes, لذا t might have happened that a servant or a الولد (in the house) or in orphan (in the house) would unexpectedly come into the oresence of the house’s owner, بينما he was on top of his الزوجة. ‘Ibn Katheer mentioned this narration in his Tafseer (3/280), and قال, “Its chain is authentic.” And لذا الله 38 ordered them to ask permission to enter خلال those three private times that He 4 mentioned. Later, الله blessed people with much sustenance and provision, لذا that they were able to put up covers and curtains, which, people began to think, were sufficient, making them no longer need the asking of permission which they were commanded with.’”"] "] Tafseer Ibn Katheer (3/303) ~ The الباب Of Noor (الآية 85). بعد relating Ibn ‘Abbaas’s narration, Ibn Katheer said, “And this chain is authentically ascribed to Ibn ‘Abbaas se.” الباب - 4 The Etiquettes Of Meeting Another مسلم 93 The Etiquettes Of Meeting Another مسلم الرسول ﷺ of الله #8 said, aed “Shake hands with بعضهم بعضاً: any hatred [that exists among you] will go away; give gifts to بعضهم بعضاً: you will end up loving بعضهم بعضاً and any enmity [that exists among you] will go away.”"1) _ And الرسول ﷺ of الله #¢ said, “No two المسلمون meet and _ shake hands with بعضهم بعضاً, except that they are forgiven . [their الذنوب] قبل they part from بعضهم بعضاً.” | The Islamic Etiquettes Of Meeting Another مسلم 1) It ls Recommended To Shake The Other Person’s Hand We learn from the above-mentioned narrations that, by simply shaking a الأخ’s hand, our الذنوب are forgiven and any hatred that exists among us will go away. النبي ﷺ #% exhorted المسلمون to shake hands with بعضهم بعضاً, and the الصحابة # applied that الهداية. Qataadah reported that he حين asked أنس 4, “Was hand-shaking common among النبي ﷺ's الصحابة 5?” He < said, “Yes,2]_ And in the story of عندما الله 3 accepted his التوبة, Ka’ab said, “I entered the " Reiated by Maalik, from ‘Ataa Ibn ‘Abdullah Al-Khurasaanee (1685). Ibn “Abdul-Barr said in At-Tamheed, “This الحديث is connected from chains that are all authentic.” He then related some of those chains (At-Tamheed المسجد, and there قبل me was الرسول ﷺ of الله . Then Talhah Ibn ‘Ubaidullah 4 stood and jogged toward me حتى he shook my hand and congratulated me.” a] And وفقاً لـ the الحديث of أنس 4, عندما the people of Yemen came, النبي ﷺ # said, Se Gold S5F AG egal JET St ws “The people of Yemen are approaching, and they have softer القلوب than you.”” [أنس 2 said,] “And they are the أولاً who came with handshaking.”"7) Al-Baraa Ibn ‘Aazib 4 said, “One of the matters that makes a greeting complete is for you to shake your الأخ's hand.’ عندما you meet your الأخ, it is السنة to shake hands with him, for doing لذا signifies an assurance of the verbal greetings of peace. The following is mentioned in Al-Adab Al-Mufrad: ‘You should know that to shake hands upon meeting [your الأخ] is an act of friendliness and an assurance or confirmation of the verbal greetings of peace. By verbally extending greetings of peace, you announce to your الأخ that he is safe from you inflicting harm upon him. Shaking hands is a pledge and an assurance to that effect. لذا by greeting بعضهم بعضاً and shaking hands with بعضهم بعضاً, each of the two people meeting knows that he is safe with the other.” بعد you have read the narrations "I Bukhaaree related it Ta’leeqan in Kitaab Al-Istidhaan, in the الباب of Hand-Shaking. Its chain is connected with Bukhaaree, from the story of Ka’ab 4 in Al-Maghaazee (4418) '9) Related by Bukhaaree in Al-Adab Al-Mufrad (967). Al-Albaanee said that, as a saying of Al-Baraa #, its chain is authentic. الباب - 4 The Etiquettes Of Meeting Another مسلم 95 that establish the importance and الفضائل of shaking hands with your الأخ, do not be miserly with yourself عندما it comes to achieving easy rewards, and do not turn away from the السنة! Question: Some people have made it a custom, بعد compulsory الصلوات, to shake the الإمام’s hand or to shake the hand of the worshipper sitting beside them. Is this practice legislated in الإسلام? Answer: It is not legislated to [customarily] shake hands immediately بعد the compulsory الصلوات; this practice is not related from النبي ﷺ #%, the rightly-guided Khaleefahs, or the other noble الصحابة 4. To apply this practice is an البدعة in the religion, and الله 3% has prohibited all innovations in the religion. Some الكتب of Islamic Jurisprudence (Figh) contain the ruling for this practice. Ibn ‘Aabideen said, “To do لذا regularly and specifically بعد the five [daily] الصلوات might lead ignorant people to believe that it is السنة to shake hands particularly on those five occasions, or that those are the most المهم times to shake hands. The reality is, غير أن, that no one from our pious predecessors applied this practice.” And this passage is in Al-Multaqit: ‘يُكره to shake hands بعد performing the الصلاة, لأن the الصحابة would not do that and لأن it is from the ways of the Raafidah (the Shiah). Shaafi’ee العلماء maintain that, بما أن it has no source in the Shariah, it is a Bid’ah practice; and they rule that one who does it should be أولاً warned not to do it, and إذا he persists, he should be severely reproached.” And the following is taken from AlMadkhal: ’'It is a Bid’ah practice. In the Shariah, it is legislated for a مسلم to shake hands not بعد الصلوات but upon meeting his الأخ. عندما the Shariah legislates a practice for a certain occasion, then that is عندما ينبغي لنا apply it. لذا one who [regularly] shakes hands بعد the الصلوات should be warned and reproached for having done an act that is contrary to the السنة.” The Permanent Council (in Saudi Arabia) issued the following ruling: “إذا one did not shake his الأخ’s hand عندما he met him قبل the الصلاة, he may shake hands with him بعد completing his الصلاة, regardless of whether his الصلاة is obligatory or voluntary, and regardless of whether his الأخ is sitting to his الحق or to his left. But إذا he wants to shake his الأخ’s hand بعد an obligatory الصلاة, he should wait حتى he finishes saying the invocations that ينبغي للمرء say بعد obligatory الصلوات. And we know of no narration that establishes the practice of worshippers greeting the الإمام بعد the الصلاة is over.”!4] Related Issue: In Al-Adab Al-Mufrad, Bukhaaree reported that Salamah Ibn Wirdaan said, “I saw أنس Ibn Maalik # greeting the people; he then asked me (عندما I was still a الولد), ‘Who are you?’ I said, ‘A freed slave of Banee Laith.’ He then patted me on my head three times, saying, ‘May الله bless you.””) Based on this الحديث, and بما أن doing لذا shows الرحمة to الأولاد and instills اللطف in them, يستحب to greet الأولاد and shake hands with them. By patting the young boy on his head, أنس # showed his الرحمة and اللطف towards الأولاد.
